sub_mechanisms: M_1: gating logits projected onto eigenbasis derived from input statistics
M_2: capacity vector projected onto the majorization polytope of a target spectrum
M_3: per-expert assignment via dual-variable polytope-projection algorithm
M_4: load-balance certificate as distance to the equal-spectrum vertex
stripped_llm_application: Router built as a polytope-projected gate: gating logits embedded in an eigenbasis, capacity vector projected onto a majorization-admissible polytope of a target spectrum; per-expert assignment via a dual-variable projection algorithm with a closed-form load-balance certificate.
novelty_claim_stripped: Eigenbasis-MoE (EMoE) and ERMoE use eigen-aligned routing but do not invoke a majorization-polytope constraint. Selective Sinkhorn implicitly enforces Birkhoff doubly-stochastic balance which is a strict subset of majorization. The polytope-projection certificate is new.
